title: Pisum quadratum
taxonomy: Pisum quadratum belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other members of the family, including its flower structure and leaf morphology. As a member of the genus Pisum, Pisum quadratum is further classified as a type of pea. The genus Pisum is part of the tribe Fabeae, which also includes other legumes. Pisum quadratum's classification helps scientists understand its evolutionary relationships and genetic makeup.
uses: Unfortunately, I couldn't find any reliable information on the medicinal uses of Pisum quadratum.
intro:
There is no information available about the plant Pisum quadratum. However, I can provide you with information about the plant Pisum sativum, also known as the garden pea.

Pisum sativum is a perennial legume plant that is widely cultivated for its edible seeds and is a good source of protein. The health benefits of Pisum sativum include its potential to help lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health due to its high fiber and antioxidant content. The bioactive constituents of Pisum sativum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pisum sativum are tea, capsules, and supplements that are used to support cardiovascular health and improve digestion.
